title: Schur's inequality, t = 1 case
informal_statement: For nonnegative real numbers a, b, c: a*(a-b)*(a-c) + b*(b-a)*(b-c) + c*(c-a)*(c-b) >= 0 (Schur's inequality, degree/parameter t = 1 case).
informal_proof_idea: Schur's inequality is not a sum of squares (it is degree 3, odd), so it needs an ordering case split rather than a single global SOS certificate. Split into all orderings of a, b, c via le_total (a vs b, b vs c, a vs c); within each of the resulting branches the expression reduces to a combination of nonnegative products (nonneg reals times nonneg reals, and nonneg reals times squared differences) that nlinarith can certify once given those product terms as hints.
formal_statement_pp: theorem schur_degree_one : ∀ (a b c : ℝ), 0 ≤ a → 0 ≤ b → 0 ≤ c → a * (a - b) * (a - c) + b * (b - a) * (b - c) + c * (c - a) * (c - b) ≥ 0

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode cbffe931), kernel_verified on the first Solve attempt: an 8-way case split on the three pairwise orderings of a, b, c (via `rcases le_total ... <;> rcases le_total ... <;> rcases le_total ...`), closed uniformly across all branches by a single nlinarith call with a shared set of product/square hints. Pairs with nesbitt_three_var as a second named-inequality target; closes the schur_degree_one item in packets/elementary/inequalities/QUEUE.md.
